description: Fix confirmed browser extension bugs: double-limiting in log merging that distorts results, and incorrect KeyboardEvent.code values for punctuation characters in key simulation.

userStories.title: Fix handleExtensionGetLogs double-limiting that distorts merged results
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want the extension log retrieval to apply the limit only once after merging, so that the merged result accurately represents the most recent logs across all sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Each log source (background, offscreen) is queried without a limit
The limit is applied only once after merge-sorting all entries
The filterOptions passed to individual sources excludes the limit field
Unit tests verify that merging with limit produces correct chronological ordering
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/extension-commands.ts, handleExtensionGetLogs function (~line 101). The bug: bgLogCollector.getEntries(filterOptions) at ~line 118 receives filterOptions including the limit, which truncates each source before merging. Then the merged result is truncated again at ~line 150. Fix: create a copy of filterOptions without the limit field before passing to each source. Something like: const { limit: _, ...sourceOptions } = filterOptions ?? {}; then pass sourceOptions to bgLogCollector.getEntries and to the offscreen getLogs call. Keep the final limit application after merging (~line 150) unchanged.

userStories.title: Fix keyboard simulation code values for punctuation characters
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want the key press simulation to produce correct KeyboardEvent.code values for punctuation characters, so web applications that check event.code work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Punctuation characters like !, @, #, $, etc. produce the correct physical key code (e.g., ! produces Digit1, @ produces Digit2)
The key and keyCode values remain correct for these characters
Named keys (Tab, Enter, Escape, etc.) continue to work correctly
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/key-press-command.ts. The deriveCode function (~lines 40-48) handles letters (KeyA-KeyZ) and digits (Digit0-Digit9) but falls through to returning the raw character for punctuation. Add a SHIFTED_PUNCTUATION map that maps punctuation to their physical key codes: {'!': 'Digit1', '@': 'Digit2', '#': 'Digit3', '$': 'Digit4', '%': 'Digit5', '^': 'Digit6', '&': 'Digit7', '*': 'Digit8', '(': 'Digit9', ')': 'Digit0', '_': 'Minus', '+': 'Equal', '{': 'BracketLeft', '}': 'BracketRight', '|': 'Backslash', ':': 'Semicolon', '"': 'Quote', '<': 'Comma', '>': 'Period', '?': 'Slash', '~': 'Backquote'}. Also add the unshifted equivalents: {'-': 'Minus', '=': 'Equal', '[': 'BracketLeft', ']': 'BracketRight', '\\': 'Backslash', ';': 'Semicolon', '\'': 'Quote', ',': 'Comma', '.': 'Period', '/': 'Slash', '`': 'Backquote'}. Named keys (Enter, Tab, etc.) already work correctly — they return the key name which is also the correct code value.
